intended_use: The ME-APDS (Magentiq Eye's Automatic Polyp Detection System) is intended to be used by endoscopists as an adjunct to the common video colonoscopy procedure (screening and surveillance), aiming to ass
intended_use_full: The ME-APDS (Magentiq Eye's Automatic Polyp Detection System) is intended to be used by endoscopists as an adjunct to the common video colonoscopy procedure (screening and surveillance), aiming to assist the endoscopist in identifying lesions during colonoscopy procedure by highlighting regions with visual characteristics consistent with different types of mucosal abnormalities that appear in the colonoscopy video during the procedure. Highlighted regions can be independently assessed by the endoscopist and appropriate action taken according to standard clinical practice.
ground_truth: Histopathology findings for polyp verification; procedure reports for adenoma classification
other_metrics: Polyp-wise Recall (PRecall1): 100.0% [100.0%; 100.0%] for histology-verified polyps; PRecall3: 99.6% [98.8%; 100.0%]; PRecall5: 99.6% [98.8%; 100.0%]; PRecall7: 99.6% [98.8%; 100.0%]. Frame-wise Recall (FRecall): 75.7% [72.5%; 78.8%] for histology-verified polyps. False Positives Per Video (FPPF): 0.0328. Adenomas Per Colonoscopy (APC) in clinical trial: MEAC 0.70 (95% CI: 0.58, 0.82) vs CC 0.51 (95% CI: 0.42, 0.60), representing 37% relative increase. Adenomas Per Extraction (APE) in clinical trial: MEAC 0.31 (95% CI: 0.27, 0.35) vs CC 0.27 (95% CI: 0.23, 0.31), non-inferior performance.
